user_query:
I have action logs and want you to turn them into a simple habit model and procedural memory. Read input/action_logs.jsonl (JSON Lines) where each line has: {"t": number (global ascending index), "session": string, "action": string, "reward": integer in [-1,0,1]}. Use only deterministic logic and the exact rules below, and write three files under output/:

1) output/habit_weights.json — A JSON object mapping each action to an object with these integer fields computed across all lines:
- total_count: number of occurrences
- positive_count: count of lines with reward == 1
- zero_count: count of lines with reward == 0
- negative_count: count of lines with reward == -1
- reward_sum: sum of reward values
- habit_score: 2*positive_count + zero_count - 3*negative_count
Include every action observed as a top-level key.

2) output/preferences.json — A JSON array of the top 3 actions (or fewer if less than 3 actions exist) ranked by habit_score descending, then by action name ascending to break ties. Each element must be an object with: {"action": string, "habit_score": number, "total_count": number, "automatic": boolean}. Compute automatic as (habit_score >= 5) AND (total_count >= 3).

3) output/procedural_memory.json — Build contiguous triplets (windows of size 3) of actions within each session (ordered by t). For each triplet occurrence, add 1 to its count and add the sum of the rewards of its three events to its reward_sum. After aggregating across all sessions, include only triplets with count >= 2. Output a JSON object with a single key "triplets" whose value is an array of objects: {"sequence": [a1, a2, a3], "count": number, "reward_sum": number}. Sort this array by count descending, then by the lexicographic order of the string "a1>a2>a3" ascending to break ties.

Constraints:
- Do not fetch anything external.
- Be fully deterministic: no randomness or time-based behavior.
- Ensure all three output files are valid JSON and contain exactly the specified keys/structures.
- Do not write any other files under output/.
